Class Tiles_Btn

  • java.lang.Object
    • java.awt.Component
      • java.awt.Container
        • javax.swing.JComponent
          • javax.swing.AbstractButton
            • javax.swing.JButton
              • Tiles_Btn
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    java.awt.image.ImageObserver, java.awt.ItemSelectable, java.awt.MenuContainer, java.io.Serializable, javax.accessibility.Accessible, javax.swing.SwingConstants
    public abstract class Tiles_Btn
extends javax.swing.JButton
    Abstract class Tiles_Btn - contains the common fucntions of all the kind of tiles to be created in the game. Ex. ('Tetragono','Kiklos','Trigono','Romvos', 'OrthogParalilogramo','Joker'). Extends JButton Swing class for suitable functionality.
    Version:
    12/12/2019
    Author:
    George Ziozas
    See Also:
    Serialized Form

    • Constructor Detail

      • Tiles_Btn

        public Tiles_Btn​(int coord_x,
                 int coord_y,
                 java.lang.String idShape)
        Abstract's class Tiles_Btn constructor.
        Parameters:
        coord_x - : the 'x' position of Tile in the GameBoard.
        coord_y - : the 'y' position of Tile in the GameBoard.
        idShape - : the spesific shape of the Tile ex. Kiklos,Romvos.

    • Method Detail

      • drawShapeOnTile

        abstract void drawShapeOnTile()
        Responsible for drawing through graphics each shape on the Tiles. Gets implemented from each class which extends Tiles_Btn.

      • defaultDraw

        protected void defaultDraw()
        Responsible for resseting the 'Tile' to its default drawing after the user clicks on it.

      • getCoordX

        protected java.lang.Integer getCoordX()
        Returns:
        The 'x' coordinate in the game board grid. 'protected' because only a subclass can implement them.

      • setCoordX

        protected void setCoordX​(int x)
        Parameters:
        x - : The x value you'd like to set in the x position of the tile in the game grid.

      • getCoordY

        protected java.lang.Integer getCoordY()
        Returns:
        The 'y' coordinate in the game board grid. 'protected' because only a subclass can implement them.

      • setCoordY

        protected void setCoordY​(int y)
        Parameters:
        y - : The y value you'd like to set in the x position of the tile in the game grid.

      • getIdShape

        protected java.lang.String getIdShape()
        Returns:
        The 'idShape' of the Tile 'protected' because only a subclass can implement them.
